Some writers have mastered spinning fantastic tales, trotting lies mystery, romance, high adventure that while barely believable still manage entertain
Others choose closer bone truth, making turn author's blurb back book whether what reading fiction fact.
Biel falls into this latter camp with debut collection linked stories, episodes from life youthful protagonist, Matthew Davis, that seem painfully real imaginary.
fictional community Riverside eastern Montana, Badlands blends traditional elements coming-of-age story with unvarnished portrayal cultural changes that were beginning permeate small-town America during Vietnam era.
While these aspects alone might make book worth reading, Biel's development other characters narrative that this collection apart.
example, process chronicling narrator's personal journey manhood, also exploring lives individuals whose successes tragedies they interact with protagonist help shape person Matthew will eventually become.
fact, Matthew's development times seems almost incidental author's rich treatment other people brings life.
such individual Matthew's best friend, Idaho Wells.
earlier tales their adventures resemble those Sawyer Huck Finn.
years pass becomes clear that Matthew doesn't have innate leadership skills cunning Tom, while Idaho darker much more tragic figure than Huck ever was.
Idaho flawed hero, fierce protector, doomed lover.
Biel imbues with incredible potential, also paints life with realism that precludes happy ending him.
Matthew's father another complex character whose struggles reconcile growing atheism with career pastor local churches impact futures those around him, especially son.
pathos brought about other characters such Monica Rose and, especially, Terry McAuliffe also serve enrich narrative draw reader even further into Matthew's world.
Although rich characterizations arguably finest elements book, strong contender dead-on realism atmosphere.
instance, almost warlike rivalry, hatred, prejudice between residents Riverside Pyramid, neighboring town, should easily recognizable anyone grew mid-America during '60s '70s.
Many readers will also remember intense polarization between certain segments teenagers which erupted into periodic battles between groups labeled "Rednecks" "Freaks."
does author ignore some even darker dangers that have always faced some young people growing such pedophilia.
just twelve stories Biel manages successfully revisit drama, rampant alcohol drug use, anti-war sentiments, rising social changes era.
Those prefer their fiction softer more far-fetched will need search elsewhere.
readers enjoy stories that portray life without sugar-coating language truth, Biel's well-written look growing turbulent time period must read.

It's become ritual: Every year mid-January, around time Martin Luther King's birthday, perfunctory network news reports about "the slain civil rights leader."
remarkable thing about this annual review King's life that several years last years totally missing, flushed down memory hole.
What viewers closed loop familiar file footage: King battling desegregation Birmingham (1963); reciting dream racial harmony rally Washington (1963); marching voting rights Selma, Alabama (1965); finally, lying dead motel balcony Memphis (1968).
alert viewer might notice that chronology jumps from 1965 1968.
King didn't take sabbatical near life.
fact, speaking organizing diligently ever.
Almost those speeches were filmed taped.
they're shown today
It's because national news media have never come terms with what Martin Luther King stood during final years.
early 1960s, when King focused challenge legalized racial discrimination South, most major media were allies.
Network national publications graphically showed police dogs bullwhips cattle prods used against Southern blacks sought right vote public lunch counter.
after passage civil rights acts 1964 1965, King began challenging nation's fundamental priorities.
maintained that civil rights laws were empty without "human rights" including economic rights.
people poor restaurant afford decent home, King said, anti-discrimination laws were hollow.
Noting that majority Americans below poverty line were white, King developed class perspective.
decried huge income gaps between rich poor, called "radical changes structure society" redistribute wealth power.
"True compassion," King declared, more than flinging coin beggar; comes that edifice which produces beggars needs restructuring."
1967, King also become country's most prominent opponent Vietnam War, staunch critic overall U.S. foreign policy, which deemed militaristic.
"Beyond Vietnam" speech delivered York's Riverside Church April 1967 year before murdered King called United States "the greatest purveyor violence world today."
From Vietnam South Africa Latin America, King said, U.S. wrong side world revolution."
King questioned "our alliance with landed gentry Latin America," asked U.S. suppressing revolutions shirtless barefoot people" Third World, instead supporting them.
foreign policy, King also offered economic critique, complaining about "capitalists West investing huge sums money Asia, Africa South America, only take profits with concern social betterment countries."
haven't heard "Beyond Vietnam" speech network news retrospectives, national media heard loud clear back 1967 loudly denounced
Time magazine called "demagogic slander that sounded like script Radio Hanoi."
Washington Post patronized that "King diminished usefulness cause, country, people."
last months, King organizing most militant project life: Poor People's Campaign.
crisscrossed country assemble multiracial army poor" that would descend Washington engaging nonviolent civil disobedience Capitol, need until Congress enacted poor people's bill rights.
Reader's Digest warned "insurrection."
King's economic bill rights called massive government jobs programs rebuild America's cities.
crying need confront Congress that demonstrated "hostility poor" appropriating "military funds with alacrity generosity," providing "poverty funds with miserliness."
familiar that sounds today, more than quarter-century after King's efforts behalf poor people's mobilization were short assassin's bullet.
1995 gets underway, this nation immense wealth, White House Congress continue accept perpetuation poverty.
most mass media.
Perhaps it's surprise that they tell little about last years Martin Luther King's life.
